title: Pipe to multiple commands with tee
command: command | tee output.txt | grep 'pattern'
explanation: tee splits output: writes to a file AND passes through to the next pipe. Great for logging while processing.

title: Process substitution
command: diff <(sort file1.txt) <(sort file2.txt)
explanation: <(cmd) creates a temporary file-like stream from a command's output. Compare outputs without temp files.

title: Parallel execution with xargs
command: find . -name '*.png' | xargs -P 4 -I {} optipng {}
explanation: -P 4 runs 4 processes in parallel. -I {} replaces {} with each input. Dramatically speeds up batch operations.

title: Redirect stderr separately
command: command 2>/dev/null    command 2>&1 | grep err    command &>file.log
explanation: 2>/dev/null silences errors. 2>&1 merges stderr into stdout for piping. &> redirects both to a file.

title: Find and execute in one command
command: find . -name '*.log' -mtime +30 -exec rm {} +
explanation: -exec with + batches files for efficiency. -mtime +30 means older than 30 days. Safer than piping to xargs.

title: Extract columns with awk
command: awk -F',' '{print $2, $4}' data.csv
explanation: -F sets the delimiter. $1,$2... are columns. Use with any delimited data — logs, CSVs, command output.

title: Watch a command repeatedly
command: watch -n 2 'docker ps --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}"'
explanation: Runs a command every N seconds and shows the output. Great for monitoring processes, disk space, or containers.

title: chmod numeric shorthand
command: chmod 755 script.sh    # rwxr-xr-x (owner: all, group/other: read+execute)
explanation: 4=read, 2=write, 1=execute. Add them: 7=rwx, 5=rx, 6=rw. Three digits: owner, group, others.

title: Change inner text objects
command: ciw  ci"  ci(  ci{  cit
explanation: c=change, i=inner. ciw changes word, ci" changes inside quotes, ci( inside parens, cit inside HTML tags. Muscle memory gold.

title: Visual block mode for column editing
command: Ctrl+V → select column → I → type text → Esc
explanation: Ctrl+V enters visual block mode. Select a column, then I to insert at the start of every selected line. Great for adding prefixes.

title: Record and replay macros
command: qa (record to 'a') → do actions → q (stop) → @a (replay) → 10@a (replay 10x)
explanation: Record a sequence of edits, then replay it. Perfect for repetitive transformations across multiple lines.

title: Marks for jumping between locations
command: ma (set mark 'a') → 'a (jump to mark) → '' (jump back to previous position)
explanation: Lowercase marks are per-file. Uppercase marks (mA) are global — jump across files. Use '. to jump to last edit.

title: Delete all matching lines with :g
command: :g/pattern/d    :v/pattern/d (delete NON-matching lines)
explanation: :g runs a command on all matching lines. :g/TODO/d deletes all TODOs. :v is the inverse — keeps only matches.

title: Named registers for multiple clipboards
command: "ayy (yank line to register 'a') → "ap (paste from 'a') → :reg (see all registers)
explanation: Vim has 26 named registers (a-z). Yank different things to different registers. "+y copies to system clipboard.

title: Glob qualifiers for filtering
explanation: (.) files only, (/) dirs only, (.mh-1) files modified in last hour, (Lk+100) files larger than 100KB. Zsh superpower.

title: History expansion shortcuts
explanation: !! = last command, !$ = last argument, !^ = first arg, !:2 = second arg, !:gs/old/new/ = substitute in last command.

title: Parameter expansion for paths
command: ${var:h} (dirname)    ${var:t} (basename)    ${var:r} (remove extension)    ${var:e} (extension only)
explanation: Built-in path manipulation without calling dirname/basename. x=/foo/bar.txt → ${x:h}=/foo, ${x:t}=bar.txt, ${x:r}=/foo/bar

title: Brace expansion for bulk operations
command: mkdir -p project/{src,test,docs}/{main,utils}
explanation: Creates all combinations: project/src/main, project/src/utils, project/test/main, etc. Works with cp, mv, touch too.

title: Suffix aliases to open files by extension
command: alias -s md=glow    alias -s json=fx    alias -s py=nvim
explanation: Type a filename and zsh opens it with the associated program. 'readme.md' opens in glow. 'data.json' opens in fx.

title: Auto-cd by just typing directory names
command: setopt AUTO_CD    # then just type: Documents (instead of cd Documents)
explanation: With AUTO_CD enabled, typing a directory name acts like cd. Combined with zoxide, you rarely need to type 'cd' at all.

title: Vim mode in your terminal
command: bindkey -v    # then: Esc to enter normal mode, / to search, v to edit in $EDITOR
explanation: Full vim keybindings at your prompt. Press Esc, then use w/b/0/$ to navigate, dd to clear line, / to search history.

title: Insert file paths with Ctrl+T
command: vim <Ctrl+T> → search for file → Enter inserts path at cursor
explanation: Type any partial path, fzf fuzzy-finds it, and pastes the full path. Works mid-command — type 'cat ' then Ctrl+T.

title: Fuzzy search command history
command: Ctrl+R → type fragments of a past command → Enter to run it
explanation: Way better than pressing up arrow repeatedly. Search by any part of the command. Matching is fuzzy — 'dock up' finds 'docker compose up'.

title: Fuzzy cd into subdirectory
command: Alt+C → type partial directory name → Enter to cd into it
explanation: Instantly jump into any nested subdirectory without typing the full path. Searches recursively from current directory.

title: fzf-git: browse branches and commits
command: Ctrl+G Ctrl+B (branches)    Ctrl+G Ctrl+H (commit hashes)    Ctrl+G Ctrl+F (files)
explanation: Your fzf-git integration adds powerful git browsing. Ctrl+G is the prefix, then B/H/F/R/S for branches/hashes/files/remotes/stashes.

title: Preview files with bat in fzf
command: fzf --preview 'bat --color=always {}'    # or use your fcat alias
explanation: Your 'fcat' alias already does this! It opens fzf with syntax-highlighted previews. Great for finding files by content.

title: Interactive rebase to clean up commits
command: git rebase -i HEAD~5
explanation: Reorder, squash, edit, or drop your last 5 commits. Use 'squash' to combine related commits before pushing. Opens in your editor.

title: Binary search for bugs with bisect
command: git bisect start → git bisect bad → git bisect good <commit> → test → git bisect good/bad
explanation: Git checks out commits in binary search order. You say good/bad at each step. Finds the exact commit that introduced a bug in O(log n) steps.

title: Recover lost commits with reflog
command: git reflog    # then: git checkout <hash> or git cherry-pick <hash>
explanation: reflog records every HEAD movement for 90 days. Accidentally reset --hard? Force-pushed over work? reflog has your commits.

title: Named stashes for clarity
command: git stash push -m 'WIP: auth refactor'    git stash list    git stash pop stash@{2}
explanation: Name your stashes so you remember what they contain. 'git stash list' shows all. Pop a specific one by index.

title: Parallel checkouts with worktrees
command: git worktree add ../hotfix-branch hotfix    # work on both branches simultaneously
explanation: Creates a separate directory for another branch. No more stashing to switch branches. Each worktree is a full checkout.

title: Blame with line range
command: git blame -L 10,20 src/app.py    git blame -L '/function auth/,+10' src/app.py
explanation: Focus blame on specific lines. -L takes line numbers or regex patterns. Find who last changed a specific function.

title: Search for when code was added or removed
command: git log -S 'function_name' --oneline    git log -G 'regex_pattern' --oneline
explanation: -S (pickaxe) finds commits that changed the number of occurrences. -G finds commits where the diff matches a regex.

title: Word-level diff instead of line-level
command: git diff --word-diff    git diff --word-diff=color
explanation: Highlights exactly which words changed within a line, instead of showing the whole line as changed. Much easier to read small edits.

title: Cherry-pick specific commits
command: git cherry-pick abc123    git cherry-pick abc123..def456    git cherry-pick --no-commit abc123
explanation: Apply specific commits from another branch to your current branch. --no-commit stages changes without committing (for combining).

title: Auto-resolve repeated conflicts with rerere
command: git config --global rerere.enabled true
explanation: rerere (reuse recorded resolution) remembers how you resolved conflicts. If the same conflict appears again, git resolves it automatically.

title: bat: view git changes with syntax highlighting
command: bat --diff file.py    bat -A file.py (show invisible chars)
explanation: bat --diff shows git changes inline with syntax highlighting. bat -A reveals tabs, spaces, and line endings — great for debugging whitespace.

title: eza: see git status in directory listings
command: eza -l --git    # already aliased to 'ls' with --git flag
explanation: Your ls alias already shows git status per file (M=modified, N=new). Add --git-repos to see repo status for directories.

title: ast-grep: structural code search
command: ast-grep -p 'console.log($$$)' -l js    ast-grep -p 'if ($COND) { return $RET }' -l ts
explanation: Searches code by structure, not text. $$$ matches any arguments. $NAME matches single nodes. Better than grep for refactoring.

title: fx: interactive JSON exploration
command: curl api.example.com/data | fx    cat data.json | fx '.items[0].name'
explanation: Pipe any JSON to fx for interactive browsing. Use JS expressions to filter. Arrow keys to navigate, 'q' to quit.

title: howdoi: instant Stack Overflow answers
command: howdoi convert csv to json python    howdoi -a reverse linked list
explanation: Fetches the top-voted answer from Stack Overflow. -a shows the full answer. -n 3 shows multiple answers.

title: zoxide: smart directory jumping
command: z foo (jump to most-used dir matching 'foo')    z foo bar (match both)    zi (interactive with fzf)
explanation: Learns from your cd patterns using frecency (frequency + recency). 'z proj' might jump to ~/Developer/Projects if that's where you go most.

title: delta: your git diff viewer
command: git diff    git log -p    git show HEAD
explanation: delta is already configured as your git pager! You get side-by-side diffs, syntax highlighting, and line numbers automatically.

title: navi: interactive cheatsheet browser
command: Ctrl+G → search for what you want to do → select → command fills your terminal
explanation: Press Ctrl+G anytime to search cheatsheets. Pick a command and navi fills in the template. You fill in the blanks.

title: Zellij modes overview
command: Ctrl+P (pane mode)    Ctrl+T (tab mode)    Ctrl+N (resize)    Ctrl+S (scroll)    Ctrl+G (lock)
explanation: Zellij uses modes like vim. Enter a mode, do your action, then Esc back to normal. The status bar always shows available keys.

title: Quick pane splitting
command: Alt+N (new pane)    Alt+Arrows (navigate)    Alt+W (close pane)
explanation: Your config has Alt-shortcuts for common actions. Alt+N splits the current pane. Navigate between panes with Alt+Arrow keys.

title: Floating panes for quick commands
command: Alt+F (toggle floating pane)
explanation: Opens a floating pane over your current layout. Great for running a quick command without rearranging your workspace. Alt+F again to dismiss.

title: Tab management
command: Alt+T (new tab)    Alt+1-9 (switch tabs)    Ctrl+T then r (rename tab)
explanation: Tabs are like workspaces. Create one per project or task. Alt+1-9 for instant switching. Rename with Ctrl+T then r.

title: Session management
command: zellij ls    zellij attach <name>    zellij kill-session <name>    zellij delete-session <name>
explanation: Sessions persist when you close the terminal. 'zellij ls' shows active sessions. Your config auto-attaches to the 'default' session.

title: Lock mode for nested terminals
command: Ctrl+G (enter lock mode)    Ctrl+G again (exit lock mode)
explanation: Lock mode passes ALL keybindings to the inner program. Use when running vim, htop, or another app that conflicts with zellij shortcuts.

title: Clean up all unused Docker resources
command: docker system prune -a --volumes
explanation: Removes all stopped containers, unused networks, dangling images, and volumes. Add -a to also remove unused images. Reclaims disk space fast.

title: Interactive shell inside a running container
command: docker exec -it <container> /bin/sh    # or /bin/bash if available
explanation: Opens an interactive shell inside a running container. Use for debugging, checking logs, or inspecting the filesystem. Exit with 'exit' or Ctrl+D.

title: Follow compose logs with filtering
command: docker compose logs -f --tail=50 <service>
explanation: -f follows live output, --tail=50 shows last 50 lines. Specify a service name to filter. Without a name, shows all services interleaved.

title: Build without cache for clean rebuilds
command: docker build --no-cache -t myapp .    docker compose build --no-cache
explanation: Forces Docker to rebuild every layer from scratch. Useful when a cached layer has stale dependencies or you changed a base image.

title: SSH tunneling for local port forwarding
command: ssh -L 8080:localhost:3000 user@remote
explanation: Forwards local port 8080 to remote port 3000. Access remote services at localhost:8080. Great for databases or admin panels behind firewalls.

title: Jump through a bastion host
command: ssh -J bastion@jump-host user@internal-host
explanation: ProxyJump (-J) chains through an intermediate host. No need to SSH twice manually. Add 'ProxyJump bastion' to ~/.ssh/config for permanent setup.

title: Fast file transfer with compression
command: scp -C file.tar.gz user@host:/path/    rsync -avz --progress src/ user@host:dest/
explanation: scp -C enables compression. rsync -z compresses too, but also does incremental transfers — only sends changed bytes. rsync is almost always better.

title: Copy your SSH key to a remote server
command: ssh-copy-id user@remote-host
explanation: Installs your public key on the remote server's authorized_keys. After this, SSH login works without a password. Uses your default key (~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub).

title: Clipboard from the terminal with pbcopy/pbpaste
command: echo 'hello' | pbcopy    pbpaste > file.txt    cat file.txt | pbcopy
explanation: pbcopy copies stdin to clipboard, pbpaste outputs clipboard contents. Pipe between terminal and GUI apps seamlessly.

title: Quick Look preview from terminal
command: qlmanage -p file.pdf    qlmanage -p image.png
explanation: Opens macOS Quick Look preview for any file type. Works with PDFs, images, code files, and anything Quick Look supports. Press Space to dismiss.

title: Open any app or URL from terminal
command: open -a 'Visual Studio Code' .    open https://github.com    open .
explanation: open -a launches a specific app. Without -a, opens files with their default app. 'open .' opens current directory in Finder.

title: Change hidden macOS settings with defaults
command: defaults write com.apple.finder AppleShowAllFiles -bool true && killall Finder
explanation: defaults read/write controls macOS preferences not in System Settings. Show hidden files, change Dock behavior, screenshot format, and more.

title: SSH to any device on your tailnet
command: tailscale ssh user@device-name    # or use: ssh device-name (if in SSH config)
explanation: Tailscale SSH works without managing SSH keys — uses your Tailscale identity. Enable in admin console. Your tsssh alias does this too.

title: Send files between devices
command: tailscale file cp myfile.txt device-name:    tailscale file get .
explanation: Direct peer-to-peer file transfer between any devices on your tailnet. No cloud upload needed. Receiver runs 'tailscale file get' to accept.

title: Check tailnet device status
command: tailscale status    tailscale status --json | jq '.Peer[] | {name: .DNSName, online: .Online}'
explanation: See all devices on your tailnet, their IPs, and online status. Pipe through jq for a clean view of specific fields.
